definition:
δῶμα, -τος, τό
 (δέμω, to build), [in LXX for גָּג ;] 
chiefly in poets and late (not Attic) prose; 
   1. a house, hall. 
   2. In LXX (and Hom., Od., x, 554; cf. MGr., terrace), house-top: ἐπὶ τ. δῶμα, Luk.5:19, Act.10:9; ἐπὶ τ. δώματος, -άτων, Mat.10:27 24:17, Mrk.13:15, Luk.12:3 17:31.†
 (AS)
